title: Bes'uliik

{{Quote|The kind of surprise that makes you look up and run for a bomb shelter. With a MandalMotors logo on the fuselage.|[[Goran Beviin]]|<ref name="Sacrifice">''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]''</ref>}}
The '''''Bes'uliik''''' was a cutting-edge [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] created by [[MandalMotors/Legends|MandalMotors]] after the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], the first prototypes of which entered service with the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], during the [[Second Galactic Civil War]].

==Characteristics==
{{Quote|Now tell me it's not gorgeous.|[[Jir Yomaget]]|<ref name="Sacrifice" />}}
A ''Bes'uliik'' was originally designed for a crew of three, or two with an increased weapons/fuel loadout, although the earliest production prototypes were flown by single [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]]. The fighter was designed to be versatile, capable of being everything from a [[Planet/Legends|planetary]]-attack [[Bomber/Legends|bomber]] to a long-range "hunter-killer". The spaceframe measured fifteen meters long, with a wingspan of a little over half its length. The hull was a plain, clean-lined "V"-shape with a squared-off tail and a dark gray finish with high-tech baffled exhausts to enhance its stealth capabilities. The four [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] and two additional weapons mountings were all located on the underside of the fuselage. The cockpit hatch hinged at the top for entry and the bottom edges for ejection, but there were also side hatches, and on the prototype, these bore the [[Mythosaur/Legends|mythosaur]] logo stenciled in a lighter gray color. Inside, the cockpit made use of advanced display technology, and control systems that could be activated simply by where the pilot was looking. The fighter was also able to tilt vertically on its tail section, to land and take off in a tiny area, approximately eight-by-four meters.<ref name="Sacrifice" />

Late in the design process, the design was modified to add a protective layer of [[Beskar/Legends|''beskar'']] armor, the legendary Mandalorian iron. The designers considered applying this as either as an outer skin or part of a laminated composite spaceframe. The initial prototype was completed with an [[Wikipedia:micronization|ultra-thin]] ''beskar'' outer skin, but there was also some discussion of a heavier layer of solid hull armor. Although this would render the fighter heavier, there were also hopes that a new alliance with the [[Verpine/Legends|Verpine]] [[Engineer/Legends|engineers]] of the [[Roche asteroid field]] would extend to a technology exchange, enabling the designers to lighten the underlying spaceframe structure to compensate, or to improve fuel economy.<ref name="Sacrifice" />

==History==
{{Quote|<nowiki></nowiki>''Shab'', a ''Mando''-Verpine assault fighter. That'll cause some sleepless nights on Coruscant.|[[Medrit Vasur]]|<ref name="Sacrifice" />}}
The ''Bes'uliik'' was originally designed as the '''''Kyr'galaar'''''. It was conceived after the Yuuzhan Vong War as a single development prototype combining all the new ideas being produced by MandalMotors designers—a necessarily economizing approach, as their energies were focused on reestablishing commercial production of existing designs. In 40 ABY, when a lode of ''beskar'' was found 100 kilometers north of [[Enceri]], it was realized that the powerful armor could also be applied to the new fighter. This was originally hinted to [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|''Mand'alor'']] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] by his [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] [[Goran Beviin]] soon after the discovery, and formally suggested by [[Jir Yomaget]] of MandalMotors when he showed Fett the new design shortly afterward. It seems to have been at around this time that the name was changed to ''Bes'uliik'', or "iron beast", in honor of the ancient [[Basilisk war droid/Legends|Basilisk war droids]] of the Mandalorians' warrior past (the name of which was ''Bes'uliik'' in the original [[Mando'a/Legends|Mando'a]]). Fett and Yomaget decided that, for export versions, the fighter's armor and capabilities would be significantly reduced, but half of the production run would be retained for "defense" of [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]] and the [[Mandalore sector/Legends|Mandalore sector]], with the full capabilities of the prototype, and possibly with even heavier armor.<ref name="Sacrifice" />

The first ''Bes'uliik'' was quickly completed and put into testing, and very soon after, four spaceworthy (and even combat-ready) prototypes were complete. At this point, these fighters were deployed as a combat flight, led by Fett in person, to destroy factories on [[Murkhana/Legends|Murkhana]] in a punitive raid on behalf of their Verpine allies—and also to display the new fighter's capabilities to potential buyers. The extent of Mandalorian confidence in the new fighter's abilities can be gauged by the fact that Fett gave Murkhana advance warning of exactly what they were going to target. At first Murkhana targeted the small group of ships with several squadrons of standard fighters. The defenders then realized how powerful the ships were, eventually having the entire fleet bombard the small group. Most of the shots ricocheted off harmlessly, allowing Fett and his team to easily accomplish the raid with little to no damage.<ref name="Sacrifice" /> [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] piloted a ''Bes'uliik'' during the [[Battle of Uroro Station]] in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]] against [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo's]] [[Anakin Solo (starship)|personal Star Destroyer]].

Several years later, several squadrons of the starfighter were contracted under the command of [[Mirta Gev]] to defend a monolith in the [[Chiloon Rift/Legends|Chiloon Rift]] controlled by two [[Columi/Legends|Columi]] brothers. Some of them were destroyed by the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]].<ref name="Crucible">[[Crucible (novel)|''Crucible'']]</ref>
